# UC-22.45.5 · Business-continuity rehearsal evidence — overdue BCP/DR exercises per business unit with signed attestation

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Resilience, Audit &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We make sure every department actually practises its emergency-recovery plan on schedule and that a responsible person signs off on each practice. If a department skips, slips past its deadline, or forgets to file the signed paperwork, you find out before the auditor — or the next real emergency.*

---

## Description

Quarterly correlation of the business-unit register against the rehearsal ledger. Surfaces any BU with no recorded rehearsal, an overdue rehearsal, a recent rehearsal missing the signed attestation, missing lessons-learned filing, or a failed rehearsal that was not subsequently re-run. Each finding row carries the BU owner email, the cadence target, days-since-last, and the change-request reference so an auditor can pivot directly to the underlying signed record.

## Value

DORA Art.24 and NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) raised BCP rehearsal from a calendar-item to a measurable supervisory expectation. Most organisations evidence rehearsals with a 'we did it' statement at year-end; that no longer satisfies regulators who specifically ask for the per-BU dated and signed attestation. This UC continuously catches drift the day a BU goes overdue or skips the signed attestation, turning the calendar item into a defensible position with named owners and signed evidence.

## Implementation

(1) Populate `business_units.csv` from the organisational design register; refresh quarterly via a Splunk scheduled search using the ServiceNow TA (or equivalent) — `| servicenow table=cmn_department query="active=true" | outputlookup business_units.csv`. (2) Populate `bcp_rehearsals.csv` from the GRC system after every rehearsal; the rehearsal lead files the signed PDF in the IRM module and a workflow appends the row to the lookup. (3) Schedule this search weekly on Friday 09:00 site-local so findings hit the steering pack inbox before the Monday review. (4) Open ServiceNow `BCP-REHEARSAL-OVERDUE` tickets per row; SLA 30 days for `medium`, 14 days for `high`, 7 days for `critical`. (5) Tee the positive-attestation table (BUs within cadence with full signed attestation) to `audit_evidence`.

## Evidence

Quarterly (and on-demand) saved search `UC-22.45.5` emits one row per business unit whose latest documented rehearsal is past the policy cadence. Output bundle: (a) per-BU row in the steering pack PDF, (b) ServiceNow GRC `BCP-REHEARSAL-OVERDUE` ticket per BU, (c) signed CSV in `index=audit_evidence` (7y), (d) positive-attestation roll-up: BUs within cadence are explicitly listed (silence is not evidence; the affirmative attestation is what auditors record).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Set `business_units.csv` to include a row `bu_id=BU-RETAIL-EU, cadence_days=180, owner=jane.doe@example.com` and ensure `bcp_rehearsals.csv` either has no row for BU-RETAIL-EU at all, OR its most-recent row has `completed_at=2025-10-01` (more than 180 days ago). The search must emit a row with `bu_id=BU-RETAIL-EU`, `finding=overdue_rehearsal` (or `no_rehearsal_recorded` if absent entirely), and `days_since` set appropriately. Repeat with a row whose `attestation_signed_by` is empty even though `completed_at` is recent — the search must emit `finding=missing_signature`.

### Negative scenario

A BU with a `bcp_rehearsals.csv` row whose `completed_at` is within `cadence_days` AND `attestation_signed_by` is non-null AND `lessons_learned_filed` is `true` must NOT appear. A BU marked `dissolved=true` in `business_units.csv` (post-divestiture) must NOT appear even with no rehearsal — dissolved BUs are correctly absent. A pilot/scope-excluded BU whose `scope_status` is `excluded` with a documented exclusion rationale must not appear.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- **Splunk:** Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (Victoria). This UC's runtime cost is trivial — both inputs are lookups, the search is essentially a table-join. Schedule anywhere.
- **Indexes:** `audit_evidence` (7y signed evidence). Optional `grc` (90d for live ServiceNow GRC IRM events between lookup refreshes).
- **Add-ons:** Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow v8.0+ (Splunkbase 1928) for the GRC IRM module — module name is `Risk Management` in ServiceNow's GRC product. Alternatively, custom REST pollers for Archer, OneTrust, Resolver, MetricStream.
- **Critical lookups:**
  - `business_units.csv` (required) — the authoritative BU register, sourced from the organisational-design system. Columns: `bu_id`, `bu_name`, `owner`, `owner_email`, `cadence_days`, `tier`, `regulated_classes`, `scope_status` (active/excluded/dissolved), `parent_org`, `established_at`. Refresh cadence: quarterly.
  - `bcp_rehearsals.csv` (required) — the rehearsal ledger. Columns: `bu_id`, `rehearsal_id`, `completed_at`, `exercise_lead`, `attestation_signed_by`, `attestation_signed_at`, `lessons_learned_filed`, `lessons_learned_doc_ref`, `change_request_id`, `rehearsal_type` (tabletop/functional/full_failover), `result` (pass/conditional/fail), `next_rehearsal_due`. Refresh cadence: continuous (every rehearsal triggers an update via the GRC workflow).
  - `bu_rename_history.csv` (recommended) — keyed on `old_bu_id` outputting `new_bu_id`, `effective_date`. Run an automated pre-process after every re-org.
  - `bcm_scope_exclusions.csv` (required if any BU has `scope_status=excluded`) — keyed on `bu_id`, columns: `granted_by`, `valid_until`, `rationale`, `compensating_arrangement`.
- **GRC integration:** the rehearsal ledger is best populated automatically from the GRC IRM system. Manual maintenance of `bcp_rehearsals.csv` is acceptable in tier-3 environments but introduces lag and human error in tier-1 — automate it.
- **RBAC:** users running the search need read access to the lookups. The lookups themselves should be writable only by the BCM team's service account (`svc_bcm_lookups`).
- **Evidence signing:** RFC3161 TSA reachable — same as the rest of cat-22.45.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

The data model is unusual for a Splunk detection: both inputs are lookups, not event streams. The trade-off is intentional — rehearsal records are low-volume and high-importance, so we want them in a versioned, reviewed register rather than a stream of point-in-time events. The work is in keeping the registers fresh and authoritative.

### 1a — Populate `business_units.csv` from the organisational register

The authoritative source is whichever system holds the company's org chart with `cadence_days` resolved per BU. Typical sources: ServiceNow `cmn_department` (with custom fields), Workday Organisation Management, an in-house OD system.

For ServiceNow:
```spl
| servicenow table=cmn_department query="u_in_bcm_scope=true^active=true"
| eval bu_id=u_bu_code
| eval bu_name=name
| eval owner_email=u_bcm_owner.email
| eval cadence_days=u_bcm_cadence_days
| eval tier=u_bcm_tier
| eval scope_status=case(active=="true","active", active=="false","dissolved", true(),"excluded")
| eval established_at=strftime(strptime(sys_created_on, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), "%Y-%m-%d")
| table bu_id bu_name owner_email cadence_days tier scope_status parent_org established_at
| outputlookup business_units.csv
```
Schedule daily 03:00. Pair with a freshness alert; the lookup must update within 24h of an org-design change.

### 1b — Populate `bcp_rehearsals.csv` from the GRC IRM

For ServiceNow GRC (Risk Management application):
```spl
| servicenow table=sn_risk_test query="u_test_type=BCP^state=closed_complete^completed_atRELATIVEGT@year@ago@2"
| eval bu_id=u_business_unit_code
| eval rehearsal_id=number
| eval completed_at=strftime(strptime(u_completed_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval exercise_lead=u_exercise_lead
| eval attestation_signed_by=u_attestation_signer
| eval attestation_signed_at=u_attestation_signed_at
| eval lessons_learned_filed=u_lessons_learned_filed
| eval lessons_learned_doc_ref=u_lessons_learned_attachment
| eval change_request_id=u_related_change
| eval rehearsal_type=u_rehearsal_type
| eval result=u_result
| eval next_rehearsal_due=strftime(strptime(u_next_due, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), "%Y-%m-%d")
| table bu_id rehearsal_id completed_at exercise_lead attestation_signed_by attestation_signed_at lessons_learned_filed lessons_learned_doc_ref change_request_id rehearsal_type result next_rehearsal_due
| outputlookup bcp_rehearsals.csv
```
Schedule hourly. Two years of history is the right retention for the lookup — older records archive to `audit_evidence` via summary indexing.

For Archer IRM, build the equivalent against the Resilience module's REST endpoint. For OneTrust, use the Compliance & Audit module export. For Resolver, the BCM module exposes a REST API at `/api/v1/objects/<form-id>/records`. For DocuSign-only environments, parse the envelope-completed webhook and look up the matched BU.

### 1c — Optional live event stream

For near-real-time updates between the hourly lookup refresh, ingest `sn_risk_test` change events into `index=grc sourcetype=servicenow:irm`. The UC SPL can be wrapped to merge live events with the lookup if your audit pace demands sub-hour latency — but at quarterly steering cadence this is rarely needed.

## Step 2 — Implement the search and alert

Walking the SPL:

1. `| inputlookup business_units.csv` — start from the authoritative BU register. The detection IS the disagreement between the register's expected state and the actual rehearsal record.
2. `where coalesce(scope_status,"active")=="active"` — exclude dissolved and excluded BUs.
3. `lookup bcp_rehearsals.csv bu_id OUTPUT ...` — Splunk's `lookup` is a per-row enrichment, not a join. For a one-to-many ledger we'd lose the per-rehearsal granularity, BUT we don't need it: we only care about the latest rehearsal per BU. Achieve 'latest' by pre-aggregating the lookup at write time: the `outputlookup` step in 1b should `| stats latest(completed_at) as completed_at, latest(rehearsal_id) as rehearsal_id, ... by bu_id` before writing. Document this in the lookup's README.
4. `eval cadence_days=coalesce(cadence_days, case(...))` — explicit value preferred; tier-based default fallback prevents a missing column from silently exempting a BU.
5. Four boolean flags decompose the finding types — `overdue`, `missing_signature`, `missing_lessons`, `failed_result`.
6. `mvappend(case(...), ...)` produces a multivalue finding so a single BU with multiple problems gets one row not four.
7. The severity ladder: tier-1 BUs with no rehearsal ever are critical; tier-1 overdue is high; tier-2/3 overdue is medium; signature gaps are medium; lessons-learned gaps are low; a failed rehearsal that wasn't re-run is high (regression from a previously-working state is worse than gradual drift).

### Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.45.5: BCP/DR rehearsal cadence evidence]
disabled = 0
enableSched = 1
cron_schedule = 0 9 * * 5
dispatch.earliest_time = -90d
dispatch.latest_time = now
alert.suppress = 1
alert.suppress.period = 6d23h
alert.suppress.fields = bu_id
action.summary_index = 1
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.45.5,reg=DORA,reg=NIS2,reg=NIST_800-53,reg=ISO_27001
action.servicenow_grc = 1
action.servicenow_grc.category = BCP-REHEARSAL-OVERDUE
action.email = 1
action.email.to = bcm-steering@example.com
action.email.subject = UC-22.45.5: $result.severity$ — $result.bu_id$ ($result.bu_name$)
```

Friday 09:00 timing is intentional: findings hit the steering pack inbox in time for the Monday review. Weekly cadence with daily-suppression-per-BU prevents repeat notification of the same BU while still letting newly-overdue BUs surface promptly.

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

### (a) Per-BU walkthrough on a known-good record

Pick a BU you know rehearsed last month with full sign-off. Confirm in the GRC IRM UI that the record is complete (rehearsal closed, attestation signed, lessons-learned filed). Then in Splunk:
```spl
| inputlookup bcp_rehearsals.csv
| search bu_id="<that-bu>"
| table bu_id rehearsal_id completed_at attestation_signed_by lessons_learned_filed result
```
Every non-null in the GRC UI should be non-null here. If any cell is unexpectedly null, the GRC-to-lookup transform is broken — likely a field-mapping mismatch.

### (b) Inverse check — rehearsals in the ledger but no matching BU

```spl
| inputlookup bcp_rehearsals.csv
| stats values(bu_id) as bu_ids
| mvexpand bu_ids
| rename bu_ids as bu_id
| lookup business_units.csv bu_id OUTPUT bu_name
| where isnull(bu_name)
```
Any row is a rehearsal record whose BU has been dissolved/renamed without history reconciliation. Trigger the pre-process from FP §1.

### (c) Coverage attestation

```spl
| inputlookup business_units.csv
| where coalesce(scope_status,"active")=="active"
| stats count as total by tier
| append [
    | inputlookup business_units.csv
    | where coalesce(scope_status,"active")=="active"
    | lookup bcp_rehearsals.csv bu_id OUTPUT completed_at
    | eval covered=if(isnotnull(completed_at) AND (now() - strptime(completed_at,"%Y-%m-%d")) < coalesce(cadence_days,365)*86400, 1, 0)
    | stats sum(covered) as covered by tier ]
| stats sum(total) as total, sum(covered) as covered by tier
| eval coverage_pct=round(100*covered/total, 2)
```
This is the steering pack scoreboard. Tier-1 should be 100% always; tier-2 should be ≥95% always.

### (d) Fixture replay

Load `sample-data/uc-22.45.5-fixture.json`. Contents: a within-cadence fully-signed BU (no row), a within-cadence missing-signature BU (row: missing_signature), an overdue tier-1 BU (row: severity high), a tier-1 BU with no rehearsal record at all (row: severity critical), a failed-not-rerun BU (row: severity high), a dissolved BU (no row), an excluded BU with documented exclusion (no row), a newly-onboarded BU within its first cadence (no row), and a renamed BU whose history is correctly migrated via `bu_rename_history.csv` (no row). Expected output: four rows.

### (e) Lookup freshness

Two lookups must be fresh; both belong on the daily compliance dashboard:
```spl
| inputlookup business_units.csv
| stats latest(_lookup_mtime) as last
| eval age_h=round((now()-last)/3600,1)
| eval target="business_units.csv"
| eval _ok=if(age_h < 168, "fresh", "STALE — quarterly cadence breached")
| append [ | inputlookup bcp_rehearsals.csv | stats latest(_lookup_mtime) as last | eval age_h=round((now()-last)/3600,1) | eval target="bcp_rehearsals.csv" | eval _ok=if(age_h < 24, "fresh", "STALE — GRC sync may be broken") ]
| table target last age_h _ok
```

## Step 4 — Operationalise

### Dashboard `UC-22.45.5 — BCP Rehearsal Cadence`

Panels:
1. **Row 1 — single-value tiles.** '% tier-1 BUs in cadence' (red if <100%), '% all BUs in cadence', 'BUs with no rehearsal recorded', 'rehearsals failed and not re-run'.
2. **Row 2 — finding table** from the SPL with drilldown to `change_request_id` opening the GRC ticket.
3. **Row 3 — BU × days-since-rehearsal heatmap.** Green if >50% of cadence remaining, amber 0-50%, red past cadence. Auditor reads this row by row.
4. **Row 4 — per-owner table** sorted by overdue count.
5. **Row 5 — 8-quarter trend** of in-cadence %. Surfaces programmatic decline.
6. **Row 6 — positive-attestation table.** Every BU within cadence with `rehearsal_id`, `completed_at`, `attestation_signed_by`, `lessons_learned_doc_ref`. This is the affirmative evidence row.

### Runbook

**On `severity=critical` (`no_rehearsal_recorded` on a tier-1 BU):**
1. Confirm with the BCM lead that the BU truly has had zero rehearsals — not an ingestion gap.
2. Emergency-schedule a tabletop rehearsal within 30 days. Tier-1 with no rehearsal is an Art.24 finding waiting to be made.
3. Notify the chief resilience officer and the audit committee within 5 business days.

**On `severity=high` (`overdue_rehearsal` tier-1 OR `failed_not_re-run`):**
1. Schedule a make-up rehearsal within 30 days.
2. Open a follow-up risk register entry pending evidence of re-run.

**On `severity=medium` (overdue tier-2/3 OR missing_signature):**
1. Chase the rehearsal lead or the attestation signer.
2. Common cause: the attestation PDF was signed but the GRC workflow failed to update the lookup. Re-run the GRC export.

**On `severity=low` (missing_lessons_learned):**
1. Lessons-learned filing is a programme-quality control, not a regulatory failure in itself. Address at the next steering meeting.

### Quarterly evidence pack (the actual auditor artefact)

A quarterly scheduled report produces a four-section PDF:
1. Per-BU cadence scoreboard (every BU with status colour-coded).
2. The signed-attestation roll-up (every rehearsal record from `bcp_rehearsals.csv` for the quarter, with `attestation_signed_by`).
3. The findings table (any BU surfaced by this UC during the quarter, with resolution narrative from the linked ServiceNow ticket).
4. The exception register (every entry in `bcm_scope_exclusions.csv` with rationale and validity).

RFC3161-sign and upload to `evidence-packs/cp-4/<year>/<quarter>/UC-22.45.5.{pdf,csv,tsr}`. This bundle is the DORA Art.24 / NIS2 Art.21(2)(c) / NIST CP-4 / ISO 27001 A.5.30 attestation rolled into one.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

### First-line triage when the result set looks wrong

Work these in order before assuming the SPL needs changes:

1. **No rehearsal events for a BU you know rehearsed** — `index=grc sourcetype=servicenow:irm earliest=-90d table=ucf_strategic_plan_test bu_id=<x> | stats count`. Zero means the GRC TA isn't ingesting the table OR the BU's GRC `bu_id` doesn't match the `bu_id` in the register. Confirm by inspecting the GRC record in the ServiceNow GRC console (or Archer Risk Manager, OneTrust GRC, or Resolver Risk Management — whichever is in scope) — the lookup key shown in the console UI is the canonical value.
2. **Permission denied / credential error from the ServiceNow TA** — `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ExecProcessor "servicenow" "permission denied" OR "401" OR "403"`. ServiceNow OAuth refresh tokens expire after 100 days of disuse and after every instance clone — both common causes of a silent feed break.
3. **NULL values in `attestation_signed_by` despite a DocuSign send** — the DocuSign envelope-completed webhook is failing or the `connected_envelope_id` field isn't mapping back to the GRC `rehearsal_id`. Validate `index=docusign envelope_status=completed earliest=-30d | join envelope_id [search index=grc sourcetype=servicenow:irm | rename docusign_envelope_id as envelope_id] | stats count by rehearsal_id`. Zero count = orphaned envelopes.
4. **API timeout on the daily GRC export** — large IRM tables (>10k rows) hit the ServiceNow TA's default 60-second timeout. Surface with `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd "servicenow" "timeout"` and increase `request_timeout` in the input or split the export into smaller per-table jobs.
5. **Fewer business units than expected in the output** — compare `| inputlookup business_units.csv | where scope_status="active" | stats count` against the BU master list in the GRC Console (ServiceNow → Business Operations → Business Units, or Archer → Enterprise Risk → Business Hierarchy). A mismatch means the daily refresh dropped rows — investigate the upstream query filter.

### Common failure modes

- **Both lookups stale at once** — usually means the daily scheduled extraction job hit auth/connectivity issues against ServiceNow. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ExecProcessor servicenow` for error rows; the most common cause is an expired OAuth refresh token on the TA.
- **`completed_at` parsing fails** — ServiceNow surfaces `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` while ISO is `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS`. Normalise at the lookup-write step.
- **A BU appears with `days_since` slightly less than `cadence_days` but is still flagged overdue** — clock-timezone mismatch. If `completed_at` is recorded in PST but `now()` evaluates in UTC, you can drift 8 hours. Always store completion dates as UTC ISO; document the convention.
- **`finding=no_rehearsal_recorded` for a BU you know rehearsed last week** — the rehearsal lead closed the record in GRC but used the new BU naming; the lookup is keyed on the old name. Run the pre-process from FP §1.
- **`missing_signature` perpetually flagged on DocuSign-attested rehearsals** — the DocuSign envelope-completed webhook isn't updating `attestation_signed_by` in the lookup. Configure the webhook → HEC → scheduled lookup-merge job.

### DORA-specific caveats

- **Critical-and-important-function scoping.** DORA's testing requirements differ in cadence between critical and important functions. Make sure `cadence_days` in `business_units.csv` is set per the function classification, NOT per a generic 'BU' classification. The classification must trace to the management body's approved Information & ICT Risk Management Framework — auditors will compare the cadence in this UC against that framework.
- **Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT).** TLPT is a separate Art.26 obligation with a 3-year cycle for significant institutions. Do not conflate it with the BCP-rehearsal cadence here. If your organisation runs both, keep `rehearsal_type=tlpt` in the ledger and exclude from this UC's cadence math (or build a separate UC).
- **Reporting.** A material BCP-rehearsal failure (rehearsal_type=full_failover that failed) may rise to an Art.18 major-incident classification if it identifies a real recovery gap. Coordinate with the legal team within the 24-hour early-warning window.

### NIS2-specific caveats

- **Essential vs Important entity scoping.** NIS2 cadence expectations may differ — confirm with the national competent authority's transposition (e.g. Germany's IT-SiG 3, France's LPM, Netherlands' CWV) which differ on testing frequency. Encode the national variance in `cadence_days` per BU, with `parent_org` indicating the regulatory jurisdiction.
- **Member-state-specific reporting.** A failed rehearsal that exposes a continuity gap may need to be reported to the relevant CSIRT under NIS2 Art.23. Track linkage between rehearsal records and incident reports.

### NIST 800-53-specific caveats

- **CP-4 control-enhancement gradient.** CP-4(1) Coordinate with Related Plans, CP-4(2) Alternate Processing Site, CP-4(3) Automated Testing, CP-4(4) Full Recovery / Reconstitution. Each enhancement requires evidence specific to that enhancement. This UC's evidence supports the base CP-4 and CP-4(1). Enhancements (2)-(4) require additional records (site activation evidence, automation logs, full-restore evidence — pair with UC-22.45.1).
- **Privacy Overlay.** If the system processes PII, the Privacy Overlay adds AP-2 / AR-3 requirements that the BCP rehearsal must specifically test PII-handling continuity. Encode this in `rehearsal_type=full_failover_with_privacy_validation` and assert the test occurred for tier-1 PII systems.

### ISO 27001:2022-specific caveats

- **A.5.30 ICT readiness vs 5.29 Information security during disruption.** A.5.30 is the ICT-specific control; A.5.29 is broader and includes communications. This UC evidences A.5.30. For A.5.29 a parallel UC tracking the crisis-comms-tree rehearsals (often run alongside BCP rehearsals) is appropriate.
- **Internal audit cycle.** ISO 27001 requires planned internal audits. The internal audit programme should cite this UC as one of the operational controls being tested — close the documentation loop in the ISMS audit programme.


## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup business_units.csv
| where coalesce(scope_status, "active")=="active"
| lookup bcp_rehearsals.csv bu_id OUTPUT rehearsal_id completed_at attestation_signed_by attestation_signed_at lessons_learned_filed change_request_id rehearsal_type result
| eval cadence_days=coalesce(cadence_days, case(tier=="1",180, tier=="2",365, true(),365))
| eval completed_epoch=if(isnotnull(completed_at), strptime(completed_at,"%Y-%m-%d"), null())
| eval days_since=if(isnotnull(completed_epoch), round((now()-completed_epoch)/86400,0), null())
| eval overdue=if(isnull(completed_epoch) OR days_since > cadence_days, 1, 0)
| eval missing_signature=if(isnotnull(completed_epoch) AND days_since <= cadence_days AND (isnull(attestation_signed_by) OR attestation_signed_by=""), 1, 0)
| eval missing_lessons=if(isnotnull(completed_epoch) AND days_since <= cadence_days AND (isnull(lessons_learned_filed) OR lessons_learned_filed!="true"), 1, 0)
| eval failed_result=if(isnotnull(result) AND match(lower(result),"fail|aborted"), 1, 0)
| where overdue=1 OR missing_signature=1 OR missing_lessons=1 OR failed_result=1
| eval finding=mvappend(
    case(overdue=1 AND isnull(completed_epoch), "no_rehearsal_recorded"),
    case(overdue=1 AND isnotnull(completed_epoch), "overdue_rehearsal"),
    case(missing_signature=1, "missing_signature"),
    case(missing_lessons=1, "missing_lessons_learned"),
    case(failed_result=1, "rehearsal_failed_not_re-run"))
| eval severity=case(
    isnull(completed_epoch) AND tier=="1", "critical",
    overdue=1 AND tier=="1", "high",
    overdue=1, "medium",
    failed_result=1, "high",
    missing_signature=1, "medium",
    missing_lessons=1, "low",
    true(), "low")
| eval next_rehearsal_due_iso=if(isnotnull(completed_epoch), strftime(completed_epoch + cadence_days*86400, "%Y-%m-%d"), "OVERDUE — schedule immediately")
| table severity bu_id bu_name owner_email tier cadence_days days_since rehearsal_type result rehearsal_id attestation_signed_by lessons_learned_doc_ref change_request_id next_rehearsal_due_iso finding
| sort 0 severity bu_id
```

## Visualization

Dashboard `UC-22.45.5 — BCP Rehearsal Cadence`: (1) Single-value tiles — '% of tier-1 BUs within cadence', '% of all in-scope BUs within cadence', 'BUs with no recorded rehearsal', 'rehearsals failed and not re-run'. (2) Sortable finding table from the SPL with drilldown to ServiceNow GRC ticket. (3) Heatmap of BU × days-since-rehearsal coloured by cadence headroom (green if >50% of cadence remaining, amber if 0-50%, red if past cadence). (4) Per-business-owner table sorted by overdue count — the steering pack assigns named accountability. (5) Trend line of in-cadence percentage over 8 quarters — surfaces programmatic decline (a sign the rehearsal calendar isn't being maintained). (6) Positive-attestation table: every BU within cadence, with `rehearsal_id`, `completed_at`, `attestation_signed_by`. This is the evidence the auditor records.

## Known False Positives

**(1) Recently-restructured BUs with rehearsal history under the OLD bu_id.** When `BU-FINANCE-EU` is renamed `BU-FINANCE-EMEA` as part of a re-org, the historical rehearsal rows still use the old bu_id and the new bu_id appears as `no_rehearsal_recorded`. Maintain `bu_rename_history.csv` keyed on `old_bu_id` outputting `new_bu_id`. Pre-process: `| inputlookup bcp_rehearsals.csv | lookup bu_rename_history.csv bu_id AS old_bu_id OUTPUT new_bu_id | eval bu_id=coalesce(new_bu_id, bu_id) | outputlookup bcp_rehearsals.csv`. Run after every re-org.

**(2) Tabletop rehearsals counted equally to functional ones.** Some regulators (DORA in particular) expect functional or full-failover rehearsals at a longer cadence but accept tabletop rehearsals as supplementary. If your policy distinguishes — e.g. functional every 12 months, tabletop optionally every 6 months — the SPL needs to enforce the longer cadence on functional/full-failover types only. Replace `eval overdue=...` with: `eval last_functional_epoch=if(rehearsal_type IN ("functional","full_failover"), completed_epoch, null()) | stats max(last_functional_epoch) as last_functional, max(completed_epoch) as last_any by bu_id | ...` and apply cadence to `last_functional`.

**(3) Quarterly cadence with annual exercise — counting the wrong axis.** A BU may run a quarterly tabletop AND an annual functional. If `cadence_days=90` is applied to the annual record, the BU appears perpetually overdue because the most recent tabletop is the last rehearsal of any kind but the annual is also valid. Solution: keep `cadence_days` policy-specific in the lookup (a single `cadence_days` reflects the shortest cadence) AND join on the longest-cadence rehearsal type for that BU explicitly.

**(4) Newly onboarded BU within the first cadence period.** A BU established 30 days ago against a 365-day cadence isn't actually overdue. Add an `established_at` column to `business_units.csv` and append `| where now() - strptime(established_at,"%Y-%m-%d") > cadence_days*86400` to the SPL for the `no_rehearsal_recorded` finding only — overdue findings on existing BUs are unaffected.

**(5) Pilot / excluded BUs.** Some BUs are formally excluded from the BCM programme (small experimental ventures, pre-IPO carveouts). The `scope_status=excluded` filter handles this. Every excluded entry MUST have a documented exclusion rationale in `bcm_scope_exclusions.csv` — open exclusions with no rationale are themselves findings.

**(6) Signed attestation in DocuSign but not yet ingested.** The DocuSign Connect HEC poller may lag the actual signing by hours-to-days. To prevent the `missing_signature` finding from firing on freshly-signed attestations, ingest the DocuSign envelope-status event stream and update `attestation_signed_by` and `attestation_signed_at` in `bcp_rehearsals.csv` within an hour of signature.

**(7) Suppression mechanism.** All exception entries live in `bcm_scope_exclusions.csv` and `bu_rename_history.csv`. Exceptions are reviewed quarterly by the BCM steering committee. Any exception unreviewed for 4 quarters is itself a finding raised to the chief resilience officer.

## References

- [Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 (DORA) — Article 24 Testing of ICT tools and systems](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj)
- [Directive (EU) 2022/2555 (NIS2) — Article 21 cybersecurity risk management measures](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2022/2555/oj)
- [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 — CP-4 Contingency Plan Testing](https://csrc.nist.gov/projects/cprt/catalog#/cprt/framework/version/SP_800_53_5_2_0/home?element=CP-4)
- [NIST SP 800-84 — Guide to Test, Training, and Exercise Programs for IT Plans and Capabilities](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/84/final)
- [ISO 27001:2022 / ISO 27002:2022 — Annex A.5.30 ICT readiness for business continuity](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001)
- [ISO 22301:2019 — Security and resilience — Business continuity management systems](https://www.iso.org/standard/75106.html)
- [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)
